item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't want the biggest city, but I wanted a big one. Never been there myself, but I feel like the actual locations could give it some reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow the first SCP set in my home town! i dont know if that is a good or bad thing though. buqt why did you set it in San Jose?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well they are dead, and mostly pretty brutally killed, some fluid and tissue loss isn't wholly unlikely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1.9m tall, 78 kg weight
1.9m tall, 71 kg weight
2m tall, 71 kg weight

As a guy who matches the first description number for number, I have a couple of questions. Do MTF Lambda-5 agents even lift? Does the Foundation even feed MTF agents? Because a guy who's 2 meters tall and weighs only 71kg is only useful in combat when used as a bludgeoning device.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a roughly 12 kilometer area

Shouldn't that be something like "12 square kilometers"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had initially downvoted when this first appeared, based on the fact that it was lacking supplements and that it employed humes1. However, with the other material supplied, I could see that this was something that was heavily context-dependent, so I went back to read again with an open mind.

As a concept and as the opening of a larger narrative, this could work. The initial pitch is one that I would say is interesting, and I found myself wanting to know more. Unfortunately, the story that followed didn't quite live up to the initial interest of the premise. As such, my downvote remains. This isn't bad, but it doesn't feel like it quite hit what it was aiming for.

Footnotes
1. This concept is so deficient in logic that I will indeed downvote for its presence unless the article is really, really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Humes… Why'd it have to be Humes?

The set up here is very good. We are given eeire hints that something terrible happened to Lamda-5, but little else.

I don't like the needless proliferation of reality stabilization technology, but not enough to affect my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn, Hawkin got all the edits. :V Well, I like skips that affect the Foundation specifically, and this is no different. The guy who showed up full of pens is probably the most interesting one; I could probably criticize this for being just over the line of "interesting enough to upvote", but then I haven't read the interview.

Interview's p. gud tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a bit of redundancy in the paragraphs that discuss the variations in the duplicates. Namely the bolded parts below:

Multiple such duplicates have appeared for single agents, however differences (namely in weight, build, tattoo patterns, etc.) have been cited between instances and related persons (See Addendum 1 for details). Most intact SCP-2446-1 manifest in an early state of putrefaction.

SCP-2446-1 tend to appear in dress similar to Foundation-issue Mobile Task Force combat gear. Style and composition of such dress (as well as worn equipment) vary with some frequency. SCP-2446-1 which do not manifest in MTF gear manifest in standard civilian clothes. Certain physical factors, like the weight of an instance, vary between duplicates, however genetic makeup never seriously differs. Clothes and gear retrieved from SCP-2446-1 also vary in style and construction.

The last two sentences can pretty much be entirely removed, since they don't add any new information.

Also,

certain reading fall outside

should be "readings".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just dropping into the discussion before i finish reading to point out that the first two instances described are really fucking tall.

Average human male height in the US is, like, 1.75 meters or something. I'm fairly tall, and I'm only about 1.83 meters tall. A 2.2 meter-tall dude is, like, Shaquille O'Neal sized. Or bigger.

ETA: Yeah, according to Wikipedia, Shaq is 2.16 meters tall. That means that these peeps are really fucking tall. If that's not intentional, fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a good start to the contest. My only gripe is that it never states the actual duty of Lambda 5, but I guess that's what the supplements are for.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. It's definitely intriguing, I'll give you that… Until the Supplementals are released, I think this is a no-vote for me. It leaves me hanging, wanting more—anything more. If that's what you intended, then good job!

Hope the rest of the posts clear up this black hole growing in my stomach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an entry to the MTF Contest, written in conjunction with blithely, TophatLord, and EmberTech.

Other contest entries are:
SCP-2446-B4 Interview
2525 North Waterworks St.
Lambda-Killer
The Lives of Charles Hull
